steps.title: Spot the step-by-step sequence
steps.content:
The passage describes a sequential process: it uses “First” to introduce the initial action, then needs a transition to the next action, then concludes with “Ultimately” for the final result.

This is a clear chronological progression: step 1 → step 2 → final outcome.

steps.title: Pick the transition showing progression
steps.content:
“Then,” correctly introduces the next action in the sequence—what happens after the first step is completed. In other words signals a restatement, Instead signals contrast, and First of all creates redundancy with the opening transition, none of which fit the forward-moving sequence.

That confirms A is correct.
